zotero.abstractNote: We propose a novel stochastic Frank-Wolfe (SFW) algorithm for composite convex minimization problems. Our algorithm uses a variance-reduced gradient estimator and a specific step-size strategy to achieve a convergence rate of O(1/k) for general convex functions and O(1/kˆ2) for strongly convex functions, where k is the number of iterations. We also provide a theoretical analysis of the algorithm's convergence properties and demonstrate its effectiveness on several machine learning problems.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 32
